tasks.title: Define canonical dataset-boundary contract and rerun validation categories
tasks.description: Establish the single canonical dataset-boundary contract for rerun validation and classify the validation result categories needed to defend deterministic rerun claims.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Batch 1 first slice is now implemented on main in commit c21c51c, including dataset_boundary.v1 claims and rerun validation categories across lineage and API payloads.
tasks.priority_reason: Completed and validated with targeted pytest coverage plus compileall, so downstream lineage and experiment tasks can now assume the canonical boundary contract exists.

tasks.title: Ship operator-visible lineage mismatch summary from rerun taxonomy
tasks.description: Use the new rerun validation categories to expose operator-visible lineage mismatch summaries so exact_match, checkpoint_match, window_match, delegated_match, mode_translation, and dataset_changed are understandable without reading raw payload internals.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit 58308b8 with lineage summary builders, API serialization through provenance.lineage_summary, control-room summary cards, and matching docs updates.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after backend, UI, tests, docs, commit, and push all landed, so queue selection should move to the remaining lineage follow-up surfaces.

tasks.title: Add normalized lineage history and ingestion-job query surfaces
tasks.description: Build the next lineage slice after the shipped summary by exposing normalized lineage history, ingestion-job history, and summary-driven filtering/query surfaces so operators can inspect provenance and rerun context without payload-heavy or shell-based workflows.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit f0bac34 with append-only ingestion job and normalized lineage snapshot recording plus standalone query surfaces for /api/market-data/lineage-history and /api/market-data/ingestion-jobs, seeded adapter coverage, and related docs updates.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after API, adapter, tests, compileall, docs, commit, and push all landed, so follow-up work should move from backend surfaces to operator workflows, retention policy, and alert correlation.

tasks.title: Consume lineage and ingestion history in control room workflows
tasks.description: Use the new lineage-history and ingestion-jobs surfaces in the control room so operators can inspect recent sync attempts, failures, validation claims, and dataset boundaries without leaving the main operational UI.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit fb583c2 with control-room data incident triage wired to /market-data/lineage-history and /market-data/ingestion-jobs, including triage focus switching and matching operator docs updates.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after the operator-facing workflow, docs, typecheck, commit, and push all landed, so the next queue head should move to the remaining alert-correlation follow-up.

tasks.title: Add multi-symbol filtering and export to lineage incident history
tasks.description: Extend the merged lineage incident history with multi-symbol-aware filtering, candidate-symbol inspection, and export support so operators can isolate and hand off incidents that span multiple symbols without scanning the full timeline.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply narrows this follow-up to multi-symbol incidents specifically: separate filtering and export remain missing even after the primary-focus policy and inline candidate-order rationale shipped.
tasks.priority_reason: This is executable follow-up work, but it stays proposed behind the backend metadata contract so the queue keeps one deterministic next task while the new multi-symbol behavior stabilizes.

tasks.title: Auto-link alerts into instrument triage focus and lineage incident history
tasks.description: Expose direct alert-to-lineage and alert-to-ingestion-history correlations so stale-data and failure alerts automatically open the exact instrument, timeframe, boundary, checkpoint, and failed sync job context in operator triage workflows.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit 0e5e259 with alert-to-instrument linking, auto-selected triage focus from linked alerts, Focus triage entry points across alert and incident tables, and merged lineage incident history in the control room.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after the frontend workflow, docs updates, typecheck, commit, and push landed, so queue selection should move to the explicit follow-ups called out in the latest main-session reply.

tasks.title: Embed normalized symbol and timeframe in alert payloads
tasks.description: Push normalized symbol and timeframe fields into runtime alert payloads so control-room triage linking can rely on durable backend contracts instead of frontend-side heuristics around run metadata and alert text.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit f16d2c5 with normalized symbol, symbols, and timeframe propagated through operator alerts and incidents plus control-room consumers preferring the backend contract over text parsing heuristics.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after backend, UI, tests, docs, commit, and push landed; the remaining follow-up is now consumer policy for multi-symbol alerts and provider payload expansion.

tasks.title: Define primary-focus policy for multi-symbol alerts in the control room
tasks.description: Turn the new normalized alert payload contract into deterministic control-room behavior by choosing and surfacing a primary instrument focus when alerts carry multiple symbols and a shared timeframe.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit a49b966 with a shared primary-focus helper in App.tsx that deterministically orders candidate instruments by risk -> live -> payload order -> lexical, then reuses that policy for alert linking, auto-linked triage focus, and merged lineage incident history while surfacing the chosen focus and candidate order inline for operators.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after control-room policy, operator-visible rationale, docs updates, typecheck, build, commit, and push all landed, so queue selection should move to the remaining backend metadata contract and multi-symbol workflow follow-ups.

tasks.title: Embed explicit primary-focus metadata in backend multi-symbol alert payloads
tasks.description: Extend backend alert payloads to emit explicit primary_symbol and timeframe or equivalent policy metadata for multi-symbol incidents so control-room consumers can stop relying on frontend-only ordering heuristics and share one durable focus contract.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit d3a0b89 with backend primary_focus metadata added to guarded-live multi-symbol alert and incident payloads, shared backend selection logic, and control-room consumers preferring the backend contract.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after backend, UI, tests, docs, commit, and push landed; the next runtime follow-up is to carry the same market-context contract into external provider delivery payload builders.

tasks.title: Carry primary-focus market context into external webhook and provider payload builders
tasks.description: Extend external webhook and provider delivery payload builders to emit the new primary_focus market-context metadata so downstream incident consumers outside the control room receive the same durable multi-symbol focus contract as the first-party API.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit 7ae6a04 with shared operator-delivery serializers that emit symbol, symbols, timeframe, and primary_focus for generic webhooks and remediation_provider_recovery payloads, plus webhook and rootly round-trip coverage.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after backend delivery propagation, targeted pytest coverage, compileall verification, docs updates, commit, and push all landed, so queue selection should move to the outbound follow-up gaps explicitly called out in the latest main-session reply.

tasks.title: Restore provider-returned primary-focus market context into normalized incident fields
tasks.description: Extend provider pull-normalization so third-party incident payloads that now echo primary_focus market context are restored into first-class normalized symbol, symbols, timeframe, and primary_focus fields instead of remaining nested provider payload details.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit 9035057 with provider pull snapshot normalization restoring symbol, symbols, timeframe, and primary_focus into top-level and targets payloads, then applying that market context across provider pull and external sync incident paths plus remediation.provider_recovery.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after targeted pytest coverage for operator delivery and application sync paths, compileall verification, docs updates, commit, and push all landed.

tasks.title: Add explicit market-context blocks to provider workflow notes and action payloads
tasks.description: Replace free-text-heavy provider workflow notes and action payloads with an explicit market-context block so provider-side remediation steps and downstream operator workflows receive primary_focus, symbol list, and timeframe in a durable structured section instead of prose-only text.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit 781a3eb with explicit nested market_context blocks added to provider workflow action payload creation and parsing plus separate Market context rendering in provider workflow notes/messages.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after targeted operator-delivery and application pytest coverage, compileall verification, docs alignment updates, commit, and push all landed; the remaining provider follow-up is vendor-native projection into each provider action HTTP body.

tasks.title: Project market_context into vendor-specific provider action fields
tasks.description: Extend each external provider action HTTP body to map the shared market_context block into the provider's native custom field, metadata, or annotation slots so outbound remediation workflows carry durable market context in provider-native schema positions instead of only the shared generic payload.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit 286a9e9 with provider-specific post-processors injecting market_context into PagerDuty custom_details, Opsgenie and FireHydrant details, and metadata for the remaining workflow providers while keeping the human-readable note/message block intact.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after targeted operator-delivery pytest coverage, compileall verification, docs alignment updates, commit, and push all landed; the next executable provider follow-up is downstream readback that restores and preserves this vendor-native structured context.

tasks.title: Restore vendor-native market_context readback into normalized provenance fields
tasks.description: Extend provider readback and sync normalization so vendor-native custom_details/details/metadata fields that now carry market_context are parsed back into normalized market context plus source provenance instead of remaining opaque nested provider payloads.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit ace4bbe with operator delivery normalization now restoring market_context from vendor-native custom_details/details/metadata blocks into normalized pull snapshots and provenance fields, with targeted operator_delivery and application sync coverage.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after targeted pytest coverage for operator delivery and application sync plus compileall verification, docs status updates, commit, and push all landed; the next immediate provider follow-up is finer-grained source provenance preservation before analytics and export consumers depend on it.

tasks.title: Preserve provider-specific source provenance for restored market_context fields
tasks.description: Refine provider pull normalization and provenance capture so restored market_context fields record which provider-native block and field supplied symbol, symbols, timeframe, and primary_focus claims instead of collapsing them into a single generic normalized source.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit ea10a84 with provider_recovery.market_context_provenance added across domain models, operator delivery normalization, and application sync so restored symbol, symbols, timeframe, and primary_focus claims retain provider-native scope and path detail such as PagerDuty custom_details.*, Opsgenie details.*, and incident.io metadata.* origins.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after targeted operator_delivery, application sync, and API pytest coverage plus compileall verification and docs status updates landed; the next immediate work is consuming this preserved provenance in operator-facing surfaces.

tasks.title: Consume provider market_context provenance in control-room drill-down and export surfaces
tasks.description: Thread the preserved provider-returned market_context provenance into control-room drill-down, analytics, and export/reporting surfaces so operators can inspect which vendor-native field supplied each symbol, symbols, timeframe, and primary_focus claim during incident triage and handoff workflows.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit c4cbf69 with control-room incident drill-down, guarded-live incident rows, merged lineage incident history, and Copy triage export all consuming provider_recovery.market_context_provenance so operators can inspect vendor-native source paths directly in the shipped UI.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after the control-room provenance surfaces, clipboard triage export, docs updates, typecheck, build, commit, and push all landed; the remaining follow-up is export productization and analytics aggregation beyond the new clipboard JSON surface.

tasks.title: Productize provider provenance export with filters, sort, and persisted history
tasks.description: Turn the new clipboard-only provider provenance triage export into an operator-grade workflow by adding provider-provenance-specific filter and sort controls plus persisted export history so handoffs and repeat incident reviews do not depend on one-off copy actions.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit 4b18617 with a dedicated provider provenance workbench in the control-room triage panel, provider and vendor-field filters, free-text search, newest/oldest/provider/severity sort, filtered export payloads, and browser-local persisted export history with Copy export and Load filters actions.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after App.tsx, controlRoomDefinitions.ts, styles.css, and operator docs updates landed with typecheck and build verification; the next direct continuation is moving from browser-local export history to a team-shared registry and audit trail.

tasks.title: Add team-shared provider provenance export registry and audit trail
tasks.description: Replace the browser-local-only provider provenance export history with a team-shared registry and server-side audit trail so export handoffs, replayed filter sets, and provenance incident reviews persist across operators and machines.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit 21d11f6 with shared provider-provenance export job, artifact, and audit records added across the backend surfaces plus control-room shared export list and history consumers, replacing the browser-local-only gap called out in the prior queue head.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after backend registry and audit storage, control-room integration, targeted pytest and build verification, commit, and push all landed; the next queue head should move to the three explicit follow-ups listed in the latest main-session reply.

tasks.title: Add provider provenance analytics, dashboard rollups, and cross-focus querying
tasks.description: Build analytics, dashboard rollups, and cross-focus query surfaces for provider-returned market_context provenance so operators can see recurring vendor-native source-path patterns, provenance quality drift, export activity, and related incident clusters beyond per-incident drill-down views.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit 8f1a947 with provider provenance analytics surfaces added across backend and control-room UI, including provider and vendor-field rollups, current-focus versus all-focus query workbench, cross-focus recent results, and Focus triage jump links.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after targeted provider_provenance pytest coverage, compileall for the new standalone surfaces, web typecheck/build verification, docs updates, commit, and push all landed; the next queue should split across the three explicit follow-ups called out in the latest main-session reply.

tasks.title: Add time-series provenance dashboards for provider drift and export burn-up
tasks.description: Extend the new registry-snapshot analytics into time-series views so operators can inspect provider drift timelines, export burn-up, and trend movement over time instead of relying only on current rollups.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit 7dbcd30 with backend window_days support and daily time_series analytics for provider_drift and export_burn_up, plus control-room 7/14/30 day selectors, daily drift tables, burn-up dashboards, and matching docs updates.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after targeted API pytest coverage, compileall verification, web typecheck/build, commit, and push all landed; the remaining explicit follow-ups are server-side saved views/reporting, threshold-based provider-drift alerting, and finer-grained hourly drill-down.

tasks.title: Add server-side pagination and deeper query UX for cross-focus provenance search
tasks.description: Upgrade the cross-focus provenance query workbench beyond the current recent-result window by adding server-side pagination, deeper query controls, and large-registry-safe result handling.
tasks.direction_relation_note: This remains a valid scalability follow-up for the provenance workbench, but it was not one of the explicit remaining gaps called out after the time-series dashboards shipped.
tasks.priority_reason: Keep this proposed behind the newer explicit follow-ups from the latest main-session reply so builtin next-task advances through the operator-visible reporting and alerting gaps first.

tasks.title: Add server-side saved analytics presets, shared dashboard views, and scheduled provenance reports
tasks.description: Productize the new provenance analytics by persisting server-side query presets and shared dashboard views, then using them to drive scheduled or exported recurring provenance reports without browser-local-only state.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply explicitly lists server-side saved dashboard presets, shared views, and scheduled analytics reports as the first remaining productization gap after commit 7dbcd30 shipped the daily time-series dashboards.
tasks.priority_reason: Promoted to ready as the deterministic next task because it directly matches the explicit remaining checklist from the latest main-session reply and can build on the just-shipped time-series windows without waiting for further operator judgment.

tasks.title: Add threshold-based provider-drift alerting on provenance analytics
tasks.description: Turn the new provider drift analytics into actionable runtime ops signals by defining threshold rules, backend evaluation, and operator-visible alerts when provider provenance drift exceeds configured bounds.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply explicitly calls out threshold-based provider-drift alerting as a remaining gap after the daily provenance dashboard slice landed.
tasks.priority_reason: This is clearly executable follow-up work, but it stays proposed behind saved views and scheduled reporting so the queue advances through one operator-facing productization slice at a time.

tasks.title: Add hourly provenance drill-down beneath daily drift and burn-up dashboards
tasks.description: Extend the daily-only provenance analytics with finer-grained hourly buckets and drill-down UI so operators can inspect intraday provider drift and export activity without dropping to raw incident timelines.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply says the new time-series view is daily-only and still lacks any finer-grained hourly drill-down.
tasks.priority_reason: This is executable follow-up work, but it remains proposed behind the reporting productization slice so the queue keeps one deterministic next task while intraday bucket semantics are scoped cleanly.

tasks.title: Add operator-facing retention and prune controls for shared provenance exports
tasks.description: Expose retention policy, prune actions, and related operator-visible safeguards for the new shared provider provenance export registry so server-side export jobs, artifacts, and audit history can be managed without shell-only cleanup workflows.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply explicitly calls out the absence of operator-facing prune or retention control UI after the shared registry and audit-trail slice shipped.
tasks.priority_reason: This is executable follow-up work, but it remains proposed behind the analytics slice so the queue advances through one immediate consumer of the new registry before shifting into operator policy and lifecycle controls.

tasks.title: Let browser-local provenance history entries promote into the shared registry
tasks.description: Add a dedicated action that promotes existing browser-local provider provenance history entries into the shared export registry so older local handoffs can be preserved server-side without rerunning the original copy flow.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply explicitly notes that browser-local history entries still lack a separate action to be promoted into the new shared registry after the first shared export flow shipped.
tasks.priority_reason: This is executable follow-up work, but it stays proposed behind the analytics queue head so the next task keeps building broad shared-registry visibility before adding migration ergonomics for legacy local entries.

tasks.title: Expand risk-aware primary-focus scoring beyond guarded-live market-data alerts
tasks.description: Generalize the new explicit multi-symbol primary_focus contract so non-market-data multi-symbol alerts use risk-aware scoring rather than falling back to runtime or live symbol order, keeping operator focus selection consistent across alert classes.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply says explicit primary_focus is now emitted broadly, but risk-aware scoring still only applies to guarded-live market-data alerts while other multi-symbol alerts fall back to runtime or live symbol order.
tasks.priority_reason: This is executable follow-up work, but it stays proposed behind external payload propagation so the queue advances through one contract-hardening step at a time.

tasks.title: Add operator action workflow for multi-symbol triage incidents
tasks.description: Connect the control-room triage panel to operator actions for multi-symbol alerts and incidents so ack, escalation, and remediation workflows can launch from the chosen primary focus while preserving visibility into the remaining candidate symbols.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply explicitly calls out missing operator action workflow after the multi-symbol primary-focus policy shipped, so this proposal now targets action handling on top of the chosen focus and candidate-order context.
tasks.priority_reason: This is executable follow-up work, but it remains proposed behind backend payload metadata and filtering/export so the queue advances through the remaining hardening sequence without splitting focus.

tasks.title: Interpret rerun lineage categories in operator-facing UI
tasks.description: Update operator-facing frontend views to actively interpret rerun lineage categories and dataset-boundary claims instead of showing opaque matched or drifted states.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit 58308b8 via control-room summary cards and validation summary rendering in App.tsx, controlRoomDefinitions.ts, and styles.css.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed because the UI now interprets the rerun lineage categories directly for operators instead of exposing only raw taxonomy state.

tasks.title: Design durable strategy registry schema and repository contract
tasks.description: Move custom strategy registration from process-local state into a durable registry contract with queryable records, repository semantics, and restart-safe identity.
tasks.direction_relation_note: This is the first Experiment OS slice in the fixed Batch 1 order from the execution board and latest main-session reply.
tasks.priority_reason: It is the registry foundation for lifecycle durability, promotion state, and normalized experiment queries.

tasks.title: Wire durable lifecycle and promotion state into strategy records and filters
tasks.description: Extend the strategy registry plan with durable lifecycle and promotion records that remain queryable across restart and flow into common experiment filters.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Matches the lifecycle and promotion durability slice listed after the durable registry in the Batch 1 ordering.
tasks.priority_reason: Should not be `ready` until the durable registry contract exists, because lifecycle semantics depend on persistent strategy identity.

tasks.title: Define normalized experiment summary, artifact registry, and export registry model
tasks.description: Plan the normalized experiment summary model plus artifact and export registry paths so common Experiment OS queries stop depending on payload-only scans.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Corresponds to the experiment summary, artifact, and export registry slice called out in the execution board and direction detail doc.
tasks.priority_reason: This remains immediate Batch 1 work, but it should follow the registry foundation so strategy identity and lifecycle can anchor common query paths.

tasks.title: Make benchmark-pack and promotion-review artifacts first-class
tasks.description: Add the follow-up slice that promotes benchmark-pack and promotion-review artifacts from loose payloads into first-class Experiment OS records.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completes the final unfinished Experiment OS item listed under Batch 1 exit work.
tasks.priority_reason: This should remain downstream because benchmark-pack and promotion-review artifacts depend on the durable lifecycle and normalized artifact model.
